title: [FEATURE] Implement Progress Details in podman pull API as occurs in docker pull API

comments.body: * https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15714
@rhatdan I would like to have your feedback here
@mheon @Luap99 Ideas?

The API already supports the same progress details as the docker compat API endpoint. Just need to add `compatMode=true` to the query. I did not check if this actually contains all the details that docker offers.

The encoding thing should be an easy fix. The other messages at the end should be possible to add with the current info I think. PRs welcome.

I don't think our backend makes any difference for "Extracting" so that info simply does not exists. No idea what "Verifying Checksum" means either.
cc @mtrmac

c/image and related repos do track a bunch of RFEs in this general area (e.g. https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/658 , https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/198 ), but short-term no work is happening in this area.

(Historically, we were limited by a frozen API of the transport abstraction, making it impossible for individual transports to contribute progress information. That’s no longer the case, we do have an option to transfer more data between the generic copy code and transports. But none of that exists.)

---

> Furthermore, there is not the checksum verification message

(and the like…) 

I don’t consider that a valid way to consume Docker’s progress API. `status` is English text for users to read, not a constant for any software to expect.

E.g. whether checksum verification is a separate phase or something happening concurrently with the transfer is an internal implementation detail, and depends on the source/destination.

What do you need these details for?

If the goal is to communicate progress _to humans_, there are various ways to structure a generic progress API to allow that (just a percentage, percentage + text (and localization!), some hierarchical structure — https://developer.apple.com/documentation/foundation/nsprogress is one of the more complex designs). Similarly, logs used for after-the-fact debugging need records of what happened, but not a commitment to any specific structure or order.

I’m fundamentally opposed to making any promises on the structure of the copy process on the requested level of detail, constraining our ability to change the implementation — at least without any identified benefit to tying out hands that way. Keeping stable APIs is hard enough when talking about the actions, inputs, and outputs; making promises about logs would be way harder.
Thanks for the detailed answer. And ok, I agree. The only thing to fix should be the `\u003e`, but I guess, as mentioned by @Luap99 , it should be a quick fix.

> What I note is that, unlike docker API, the \u003e is not decoded as >.

`\u003e` is a valid JSON encoding. If a consumer has difficulties with that, that consumer needs to be fixed to parse JSON correctly, either way.

The producer can produce either one, and I wouldn’t object to a PR that changes this just to save some trivial amount of bandwidth, but either way the parser _needs to_ be able to parse JSON as specified ( https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/0342576056e80459c18cd78a2cdbf6f29961fe20/pkg/jsonmessage/jsonmessage.go#L145 allows for arbitrary JSON fields with arbitrary types to be included); we are not going to be adding special _commitments_ to avoid valid JSON values for the benefit of incorrect parsers.

Thank you for the answers. On my use case, that was to show a human readable download and extraction phase:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8ecbbced-f56a-4e46-b28b-d91fe319c641)

the download progress works well, thanking your suggestions about `compatMode=True`.

The only missing information is the Extraction, that will stuck at that value, even when download progress bar ended, because, of course, podman API does not provide that info. But I also understand that touching APIs and keeping them stable is hard.
xref https://github.com/containers/bootc/pull/921 as well as https://github.com/osbuild/osbuild/pull/1545
Just started with Podman on Windows. Is this progress bar something being worked on or rather not? I'd like to avoid questions by my colleagues who are supposed to switch to Podman, too ;)
@flouthoc is working on a design for how this will work at present

> Just started with Podman on Windows. Is this progress bar something being worked on or rather not? I'd like to avoid questions by my colleagues who are supposed to switch to Podman, too ;)

Hi @jankap , are you trying to use API to get pull progress ? or it's just that on your windows CLI you can't see the pull progress ?
Hi @flouthoc it's not about the API, just using the CLI podman.exe.

@jankap Okay thanks, this issue specifically deals with API but thanks for reporting. Maintainers are aware with the issue CLI users are facing on `macOS` and `windows` and I think it will be fixed soon 😄
Podman 5.6.0 still cannot show progress bar like Linux on `macOS` and `Windows` when pulling image and so on...

> [@jankap](https://github.com/jankap) Okay thanks, this issue specifically deals with API but thanks for reporting. Maintainers are aware with the issue CLI users are facing on `macOS` and `windows` and I think it will be fixed soon 😄

There will be a new major update for the API version in the near future?

I don't expect this to require a major version bump in the API - we'll add a new option to the API that, if set to true, will enable streaming of progress information. The CLI will always set this option when it has a new enough server, so Podman itself will always have progress bars for a new enough client/server combo

> I don't expect this to require a major version bump in the API - we'll add a new option to the API that, if set to true, will enable streaming of progress information. The CLI will always set this option when it has a new enough server, so Podman itself will always have progress bars for a new enough client/server combo

Good! So this change can also apply to Podman client running on Windows and macOS? If so that would be great, because Podman doesn't show the same fancy progress bar on Windows and macOS as it does on Linux...
